title: Digital Transformation Notes | May 2024 | Hogan Lovells - JDSupra
text: In May 2024, the AI Act was finalized by the European Parliament, introducing new rules for AI, including exemptions for open-source models unless high-risk. Singapore amended its Payment Services Act, enhancing consumer protection in digital finance. The AI Index Report 2024 by Stanford University sheds light on AI's growing impact and public concern. The U.S. moves towards a national privacy law with the American Privacy Rights Act draft, aiming to consolidate data protection.

title: Microsoft Launches Phi-2: A Small Yet Mighty AI Model
text: Microsoft's Phi-2, a compact AI model with 2.7 billion parameters, outperforms larger models in coding tasks. Building on Phi-1 and Phi-1.5's successes, it's available on Azure AI Studio and Hugging Face for research. Phi-2's training used quality data and synthetic datasets, surpassing Meta's Llama 2-7b and Mistral-7B in benchmarks, highlighting its potential for AI safety and interpretability research.

title: The UK CMA's review into AI Foundation Models
text: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) investigates AI Foundation Models (FMs), focusing on antitrust risks and the sector's evolution. With 330+ FMs, the CMA highlights the role of open-source models in promoting competition. Concerns include data access, computing power, and first-mover advantages potentially stifling innovation. The CMA aims to maintain a competitive, innovative landscape for FMs, addressing challenges like interoperability and vertical integration.

title: What If Generative AI Turned To Be A Flop In Healthcare?
text: The article delves into the repercussions of generative AI not meeting healthcare expectations, highlighting potential failures like lack of evidence, absence of safety trials, and the risk of deepfakes eroding trust in medical information. It also discusses the possible broader impacts, including loss of public trust, regulatory challenges, and a dip in investment. Despite these risks, the author maintains optimism about generative AI in healthcare, stressing its accessibility and the importance of managing its swift, unregulated growth to prevent negative outcomes.

title: Grok AI | X's Latest Chatbot
text: Grok AI, launched by X (formerly Twitter), is a state-of-the-art chatbot using generative AI to create content from user prompts, including code and translations. Inspired by JARVIS and the Hitchhiker's Guide, it adds humor to responses. The latest update, Grok-1.5, shows enhanced math and coding skills, and a new version, Grok-1.5V, tests image and document analysis. Exclusive to X Premium subscribers, it competes with models like ChatGPT, evolving to refine its capabilities and accuracy.

title: Generative AI in EMR Documentation | EMRFinder Blog
text: Generative AI is revolutionizing EMR Software by automating clinical documentation, enhancing productivity, and accuracy. It allows healthcare providers to focus more on patient care, potentially reducing burnout. By evaluating medical data, it produces natural-sounding writing, eliminating manual entry and enabling comprehensive charting. Ambient AI scribe technology further reduces after-hours documentation work. The potential of generative AI in clinical documentation is vast, promising improved quality, reduced administrative burdens, and increased efficiency.
